patterns: what should i eat for breakfast
breakfast.intro:
🌅 **Breakfast Recommendations**

Breakfast is important for blood sugar control! Here are diabetes-friendly options:
considerations: Start your day with protein to prevent mid-morning crashes
Avoid sugary cereals and white bread

items: 🥣 Oats with nuts and small fruit portion

breakfast.followUp: Based on this breakfast, for lunch I'd recommend something with more vegetables and a different protein source to balance your day.

patterns: what should i eat for lunch
lunch.intro:
☀️ **Lunch Recommendations**

Lunch should be balanced to power you through the afternoon without energy crashes:
considerations: This is typically your largest meal
Avoid heavy carbs if sedentary afternoon

balancing.highCarb: Your breakfast was carb-heavy, so let's focus on protein and vegetables for lunch.
balancing.highProtein: Good protein at breakfast! Lunch can include moderate carbs with plenty of vegetables.
balancing.lightBreakfast: Since breakfast was light, a more substantial lunch will keep your energy stable.

swallow_based: 🍚 Amala with ewedu and fish (small amala portion)
rice_based: 🍚 Jollof rice (small portion) with vegetables and grilled fish
non_carb_heavy: 🥗 Vegetable stew with small portion of protein
🍲 Fish pepper soup with minimal swallow

lunch.followUp: For dinner, I'll suggest something lighter since that's closer to bedtime. Avoid heavy carbs in the evening for better overnight blood sugar.

patterns: what should i eat for dinner
dinner.intro:
🌙 **Dinner Recommendations**

Keep dinner light for better overnight blood sugar and sleep:
considerations: Eat at least 2-3 hours before bedtime
Focus on vegetables and lean protein

balancing.needVegetables: You've been light on vegetables today - dinner should include plenty of veggies.
balancing.needProtein: Let's include good protein in dinner to round out your day.
balancing.hadHeavyDay: Since lunch was substantial, keep dinner light - soup-based options work great.

light: 🍲 Vegetable soup with small fish/chicken (no swallow)
moderate: 🍚 Small portion wheat fufu with vegetable soup

lateNight.response:
⚠️ **Late Night Eating Tips:**

Eating close to bedtime can affect blood sugar. If you must eat late:

• Keep it VERY light
• Focus on protein (eggs, small fish)
• Avoid carbs completely
• Just vegetables if possible

**Best late-night options:**
• 🥚 Boiled egg with cucumber
• 🥗 Vegetable salad
• 🍲 Light vegetable soup (no swallow)

Try to avoid this pattern regularly - it affects overnight glucose.

dinner.followUp: Remember to check your blood sugar before bed if you ate a heavier dinner. For tomorrow's breakfast, start light to balance tonight's meal.

snack.intro:
🥜 **Diabetes-Friendly Snacks**

Smart snacking keeps blood sugar stable between meals:
rules: Keep snacks to 15-20g carbs or less
Include protein or fat to slow glucose release
Don't snack out of boredom - eat if genuinely hungry

nutsBased.title: 🥜 **Nuts & Seeds** (best option)
nutsBased.note: Nuts are great - high in protein, healthy fats, low in carbs!

proteinBased.title: 🥚 **Protein Snacks**
proteinBased.note: Protein snacks satisfy hunger without spiking blood sugar.

vegetableBased.title: 🥬 **Vegetable Snacks** (unlimited!)
vegetableBased.note: You can eat these freely - very low impact on blood sugar!

fruitBased.title: 🍎 **Fruits** (portion controlled)
fruitBased.note: ⚠️ Limit fruit portions due to natural sugars. Avoid mangoes, pineapples, bananas.

avoid.title: ❌ **Avoid These Snacks:**
items: Chin chin, puff puff (fried, high carb)

withMealContext.afterHighCarbMeal: Since your last meal was carb-heavy, stick to protein or vegetable snacks to avoid blood sugar spikes.
withMealContext.afterLightMeal: Your meal was light, so a slightly more substantial snack (like eggs or nuts with vegetables) would be good.
withMealContext.beforeMeal: If your next meal is soon (within 1-2 hours), just have water or vegetables to not spoil your appetite.

lowGI.intro:
📊 **Low-GI Nigerian Foods**

Low Glycemic Index (GI) foods release glucose slowly, helping keep blood sugar stable. Here are your best options:

explanation.what:
**What is Glycemic Index (GI)?**

GI measures how quickly foods raise blood sugar:
• **Low GI (55 or less)**: Slow release ✅ BEST
• **Medium GI (56-69)**: Moderate release ⚠️ OK in moderation
• **High GI (70+)**: Fast release ❌ Limit these
explanation.why:
**Why Low-GI matters for diabetes:**
• Prevents blood sugar spikes and crashes
• Helps you feel full longer
• Better energy throughout the day
• Easier weight management

grains_swallows.title: 🍚 **Grains & Swallows**
grains_swallows.tips: Portion control matters even for low-GI options! A large portion of low-GI food can still raise blood sugar significantly.

proteins.title: 🍖 **Proteins** (Most are very low GI!)
proteins.tips: Proteins have almost no impact on blood sugar! Include protein with every meal.

vegetables.title: 🥬 **Vegetables** (Almost all are low GI!)
vegetables.tips: Load up on vegetables! They fill you up without raising blood sugar.

soups.title: 🍲 **Soups** (Mostly low GI when made traditionally)
soups.tips: Nigerian soups are generally diabetes-friendly - it's the swallow that causes issues!

fruits.title: 🍎 **Fruits** (Be careful - natural sugars!)
fruits.tips: Even low-GI fruits should be eaten in moderation. Pair with protein (like nuts) to slow glucose release.

practicalTips: • White rice → Ofada rice or small portion
• White bread → Whole wheat or oats
• Sugary drinks → Water or unsweetened tea
lowGI.followUp: Would you like specific meal recommendations using these low-GI foods? I can suggest complete meals based on your preferences!

bloodSugar.intro:
📊 **Blood Sugar Management Through Diet**

Managing blood sugar is all about what, when, and how much you eat:

generalTips.title: **Daily Habits for Stable Blood Sugar:**
tips: 1️⃣ **Eat regular meals** - Don't skip meals, especially breakfast. Skipping causes blood sugar swings.
2️⃣ **Choose low-GI foods** - Ofada rice, amala, wheat fufu instead of white rice, eba, pounded yam.
3️⃣ **Add protein to meals** - Fish, beans, eggs help slow glucose absorption.
4️⃣ **Load up on fiber** - Vegetables, okra, beans help stabilize blood sugar.
5️⃣ **Control portions** - Even healthy foods raise blood sugar if eaten in large amounts.
6️⃣ **Time your meals** - Eat at consistent times daily. Dinner at least 2-3 hours before bed.
7️⃣ **Stay hydrated** - Drink plenty of water. Dehydration affects blood sugar levels.

mealTiming.title: **Optimal Meal Timing:**
schedule: 🌙 **Dinner**: 4-5 hours after lunch, 2-3 hours before bed
mealTiming.tips: Consistent meal times help your body regulate blood sugar better.

portionControl.title: **Portion Guidelines:**
portionControl.tips: The plate method: 1/2 vegetables, 1/4 protein, 1/4 carbs

beforeMeals.title: **Before Eating:**
tips: • Check blood sugar if possible
• Drink water first
• Start with vegetables or soup
• Eat protein before carbs when possible

afterMeals.title: **After Eating:**
tips: • Take a 10-15 minute walk (reduces blood sugar by 20-30%!)
• Avoid lying down immediately
• Monitor how you feel
• Check blood sugar after 2 hours if possible

highBloodSugar.title: ⚠️ **If Blood Sugar is High:**
tips: • Drink water to help flush glucose
• Avoid carbs until levels normalize
• Take a walk to help lower it
• Eat vegetables or lean protein only
• Contact healthcare provider if very high or persistent

lowBloodSugar.title: ⚠️ **If Blood Sugar is Low (Hypoglycemia):**
tips: • Eat 15-20g fast-acting carbs immediately:
- 3-4 glucose tablets
- 1/2 cup fruit juice
- 1 tablespoon honey
- 3-4 pieces of hard candy
• Wait 15 minutes and recheck
• Follow with a balanced snack
• Seek medical help if it doesn't improve

foodsToEnjoy.title: ✅ **Blood Sugar Friendly Foods:**
items: • Garden egg and other low-GI fruits

bloodSugar.followUp: Use the meal recommendation feature for personalized suggestions based on your profile!

diabetes.intro:
💪 **Living Well with Diabetes**

Diabetes doesn't mean giving up delicious food - it means making smarter choices!

mindset.title: **Healthy Mindset:**
points: • It's about MANAGING, not curing - and you CAN manage it well!
• You can still eat delicious Nigerian food
• Small, consistent changes beat dramatic diets

howIHelp.title: **How Gluvia Helps You:**
features: 🍽️ **Meal Recommendations** - Tap the meal button for personalized food suggestions based on YOUR profile
📊 **Food Database** - I have {foodCount}+ Nigerian foods with full nutrition data including glycemic index
⚖️ **Dietary Rules** - I apply {ruleCount} rules to ensure recommendations are safe for your condition
💰 **Budget Aware** - I consider your budget when recommending meals
⚠️ **Allergy Safe** - I exclude foods you're allergic to

dailyHabits.title: **Daily Habits That Help:**
afternoon: • Take a short walk after eating
evening: • Eat dinner early (2-3 hrs before bed)

exercise.title: **Physical Activity:**
tips: • 30 minutes of walking daily helps tremendously
• Walk after meals to lower blood sugar
• Any movement is better than none
• Start small and build up

diabetes.motivation:
**Remember:** You're not alone in this journey. Millions of people manage diabetes successfully through diet and lifestyle. I'm here to help you do the same! 💚

What would you like help with today?
diabetes.followUp: Would you like meal recommendations, or do you have specific questions about managing your diabetes?

nigerianFood.intro:
🇳🇬 **Nigerian Foods & Diabetes**

Nigerian cuisine can absolutely be diabetes-friendly! It's all about choosing wisely:

swallows.title: 🍚 **Swallows (Ranked Best to Worst for Diabetes)**
good: ✅ Wheat fufu (semovita) - Lower GI, higher fiber
✅ Amala (yam flour) - Moderate GI, traditional option
✅ Oat fufu - Excellent, high fiber, low GI
caution: ⚠️ Pounded yam - Higher GI, limit portions
avoid: ❌ Eba/garri - Very high GI, spikes blood sugar fast
❌ Fufu (cassava) - High GI, similar to eba
swallows.tips: Always keep swallow portions small (fist-sized) and load up on soup/vegetables.

soups.title: 🍲 **Soups (Most are diabetes-friendly!)**
excellent: ✅ Ewedu - Very low carb, high fiber
✅ Okra soup - Low carb, good fiber
good: ✅ Egusi soup - Good protein, healthy fats
moderate: ⚠️ Ogbono soup - Can be carb-heavy with thickeners
soups.tips: Soups are your friend! Make soup the star, swallow the supporting actor.

rice_dishes.title: 🍚 **Rice Dishes**
better_options: ✅ Ofada rice - Lower GI, more nutritious
✅ Brown rice - Higher fiber, moderate GI
✅ Rice with lots of vegetables - Stretches the rice
eat_less: ⚠️ Jollof rice - Okay in small portions
⚠️ White rice and stew - Smaller portions
rice_dishes.tips: Portion is key! Use more vegetables/protein, less rice. One serving = fist-sized.

proteins.title: 🍖 **Proteins (Great for diabetes!)**
proteins.tips: Include protein with every meal to slow glucose absorption.

nigerianFood.statistics: I have **{foodCount}+ Nigerian foods** in my database with detailed nutrition information!
nigerianFood.followUp: Want me to recommend specific meals, or ask about any Nigerian food!

low.label: low GI ✅
low.advice:
**Great choice!** This is a diabetes-friendly option.

• Releases glucose slowly
• Helps maintain stable blood sugar
• Good for regular consumption

**Tip:** Even low-GI foods should be eaten in appropriate portions.
low.portion: You can enjoy this regularly as part of a balanced meal.

medium.label: medium GI ⚠️
medium.advice:
**Okay in moderation.** This food has a moderate impact on blood sugar.

• Raises blood sugar at a moderate rate
• Best paired with protein or vegetables
• Watch your portions

**Tip:** Combine with low-GI foods and protein to reduce impact.
medium.portion: Enjoy in smaller portions and pair with protein or vegetables.

high.label: high GI ❌
high.advice:
**Use caution.** This food can raise blood sugar quickly.

• Rapid glucose release
• Can cause blood sugar spikes
• Better alternatives exist

**Tip:** If you eat this, keep portions very small and always pair with protein/fiber.
high.portion: Consider smaller portions or healthier alternatives. If eating, combine with protein and vegetables.

default.intro: I'm **Gluvia AI**, your personal nutrition assistant for diabetes management! 🥗
features: **Here's what I can help you with:**

1. 🍽️ **Meal Recommendations**
   Tap the meal button for personalized breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack suggestions based on your health profile.

2. 📚 **Food Information**
   Ask me about any Nigerian food - I'll tell you its nutrition facts and if it's diabetes-friendly.

3. 💡 **Blood Sugar Tips**
   Get practical advice for managing blood sugar through diet and lifestyle.

4. 🔍 **Food Safety Questions**
   Ask "Can I eat [food]?" to know if something is safe for diabetes.
default.stats: 📊 I have **{foodCount}+ Nigerian foods** and **{ruleCount} dietary rules** in my database to give you the best recommendations!
default.cta: Or tap the 🍽️ **meal button** below to get started with personalized recommendations!
